title: 10 Returning AFT Transactions on ORS
body_text:
10	Returning AFT Transactions on ORS
10.1	Overview of Returning AFT Transactions on ORS
During enrollment to ORS, your financial institution determines if it will enter AFT returns directly on ORS or upload files to ORS. This Chapter describes how to dishonour and return AFT transactions directly on ORS. For procedures on uploading AFT return files or AFT Notice of Change files to Central 1, see Chapter 15, Uploading Files to ORS. Find additional information on returning AFT transactions in the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part.
Once a returned AFT transaction is entered on ORS or a file of returned AFT transactions is uploaded to ORS, Central 1 forwards the dishonoured AFT transactions to the originating financial institution. Although AFT transactions can be viewed on ORS for 93 calendar days, ORS doesnât permit the return of transactions more than 90 calendar days after the processing date.
Central 1 processes AFT returns entered on ORS or uploaded to ORS three times a day â 10:00 am, 4:00 pm, and 11:00 pm PT (1:00 pm, 7:00 pm, and 2:00 am ET).
Caution
:	This chapter describes how to return AFT transactions on ORS. It doesnât provide detailed information on Payments Canada rules. See the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part for more information. Your financial institution is responsible for complying with all Payments Canada rules and time frames.
Related Information
Chapter 14
, Uploading Files to ORS
Section 1.2
, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive
10.2	Identifying AFT Transactions Eligible for Return on ORS
The ORS search results are colour-coded to help users identify AFT transactions that are eligible for return. The colour code is displayed under the A column. See Figure 1.
Note:	The eligibility colour code describes whether the item selected for return will be processed during a regular return cycle or with Payments Canada extended time frames. Service window colour codes donât apply to AFT transactions. See Section 1.2, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive, for more information.
Figure 1: Returned Item Colour Codes
Green (next day)
â
Items can be returned with reason codes that have a time frame of the next business day.
Red (extended return)
â Items can only be returned following Payments Canada rules for extended time frames (i.e., only specific return reason codes are allowed). ORS enforces compliance with these rules.
Related Information
Appendix #A-6
, AFT Return Reason Codes
10.3	Creating AFT Returns on ORS
This section provides technical procedures on returning AFT transactions via ORS. Find information on the business rules associated with AFT returns in the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part.
Proceed as follows to return an AFT transaction on ORS:
Locate the AFT transaction that you want to return. See Section 7.5, Searching for AFT Transactions on ORS.
Select the
E
link in the A column beside the AFT transaction that you want to return. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Search Results Page
Note:	The E link is also available on the Search View page.
Enter a 3-digit return reason code or select a return reason from the drop-down menu. See Figure 3.
Important
:	See Appendix #A-6, AFT Return Reason Codes, for a summary of the Payments Canada return time frames and business rules that apply to each return reason code. Some return reasons require a reimbursement claim. See the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part for more information.
Figure 3: Edit AFT Return Page
Select
Submit
. The return status appears in the RI column. See Figure 4.
Note:	The status of the AFT transaction will be displayed on the Search View page if this is the page from which the E link was selected.
Figure 4: Search Results with AFT Return Information
P
= Pending
C
= Complete â The status changes to C when Central 1 accepts the item at the next processing time â 10:00 am, 4:00 pm, or 11:00 pm PT (1:00 pm, 7:00 pm, or 2:00 am ET). For further clarification, the TF column displays R1, R2, R3 to indicate when Central 1 processed the item.
R1
= First Processing window â 10:00 am PT (1:00 pm ET)
R2
= Second Processing window â 4:00 pm PT (7:00 pm ET)
R3
= Third Processing window â 11:00 pm PT (2:00 am ET)
Related Information
Appendix #A-6
, AFT Return Reason Codes
10.4	Editing AFT Returns on ORS
AFT return information (e.g., the return reason code) can be edited on ORS during the regular service window. Only AFT items with an RI column status of Pending (P) can be edited. If the RI column has a status of Complete (C), the item has been returned to the originating financial institution and the E link isnât available.
Proceed as follows to edit an AFT return on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Summary Option
Select the
aft
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: AFT Option
Select the
E
link beside the AFT transaction that you want to edit. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Return Summary â AFT
Note:	The E link is also available from the Search Results or Search View pages.
Make the necessary changes (e.g., select a different return reason code). See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Edit AFT Return
Select
Submit
to save the change.
10.5	Viewing a Summary of AFT Returns on ORS
Proceed as follows to view a summary of AFT transactions returned on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Summary Option
Select the
aft
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: AFT Option
View the AFT transactions selected for return on the current date. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Return Summary â AFT
10.6	Cancelling AFT Returns on ORS
AFT returns can be cancelled on ORS during the regular service window. Only AFT items with an RI column status of Pending (P) can be cancelled. If the RI column has a status of Complete (C), the item has been returned to the originating financial institution and the E link isnât available.
Proceed as follows to cancel an AFT return on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Summary Option
Select the
aft
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: AFT Option
Select on the
E
link beside the AFT return that you want to cancel. See Figure 14.
Note:	The E link is also available from the Search Results and Search View pages.
Figure 14: Return Summary â AFT
Select
Delete
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Delete Return
10.7	Settlement of Returned AFT Transactions
Canadian dollar AFT returns are debited and credited to your financial institutionâs Canadian dollar Central 1 account. US dollar AFT returns are debited and credited to your financial institutionâs US dollar Central 1 account. See the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part for more information.
Review the Batch Returns User List (ICBR/UCBR), the Settlement Register (ICSR/UCSR), or the Incoming AFT Transaction List (ICTI/UCTI) the day after the returned AFT transactions are submitted to confirm that they were processed. Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICBR, ICSR, and ICTI. US dollar transactions are reported on the UCBR, UCSR, and UCTI. Notify Central 1 immediately if a discrepancy is identified.
END
